mission.title: envoy: Peak EWMA load balancing
mission.description: Peak EWMA load balancing. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: *I would like envoy to support peak EWMA load balancing, in addition to the existing load balancing algorithms.*

*Description*:

Please correct me if this is already supported.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Peak EWMA load balancing
steps.description:
Commit Message: PeakEWMA load balancer
Additional Description:
An improved PeakEWMA load balancer implementation, using P2C(Power of 2 Choices) with weighted active request and error rate fixed latency. The score of a host can be expressed by the following formula (the lower, the better)

$$
Score
```yaml
![Screen Shot 2024-03-27 at 18 00 06](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/assets/20726865/be3adedc-cdfc-48e6-94fd-0c51b7aa3f61)


Here is a graph showing the response time per pod:
Query is:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Peak EWMA load balancing" is functioning correctly.
